akiec.name: Actinic Keratosis
akiec.description: Actinic keratosis is a rough, scaly patch on the skin caused by years of sun exposure. It is considered a pre-cancerous condition because it may develop into squamous cell carcinoma if untreated.
symptoms: Small crusted area on sun-exposed skin
causes: Fair skin with poor UV protection
akiec.doctor_visit: Consult a dermatologist if the lesion changes size, color, or begins to bleed.

bcc.name: Basal Cell Carcinoma
bcc.description: Basal cell carcinoma is the most common form of skin cancer. It grows slowly and rarely spreads, but it can damage nearby tissues if untreated.
symptoms: Bleeding or scabbing sore that heals and returns
bcc.doctor_visit: Immediate dermatological evaluation is recommended for any suspicious lesion.

bkl.name: Benign Keratosis
bkl.description: Benign keratosis includes harmless skin growths such as seborrheic keratosis and solar lentigo. They are non-cancerous and often appear with age.
bkl.doctor_visit: Consult a doctor if the lesion changes rapidly or becomes painful.

df.name: Dermatofibroma
df.description: Dermatofibromas are common benign skin nodules often found on the legs or arms. They are harmless and rarely require treatment.
symptoms: Small firm bump under the skin
treatment: Surgical removal if painful or bothersome
df.doctor_visit: Seek medical advice if the lesion grows rapidly.

mel.name: Melanoma
mel.description: Melanoma is the most dangerous form of skin cancer. It develops in the pigment-producing cells and can spread rapidly if untreated.
symptoms: Multiple colors in a single mole
mel.doctor_visit: Urgent dermatological consultation is required if melanoma is suspected.

nv.name: Melanocytic Nevus
nv.description: Melanocytic nevi are common moles that are generally harmless but should be monitored for changes.
nv.doctor_visit: Consult a doctor if the mole changes shape, color, or size.

vasc.name: Vascular Lesion
vasc.description: Vascular lesions are abnormalities in blood vessels that appear as red or purple spots on the skin.
vasc.doctor_visit: Seek medical advice if lesions grow rapidly or bleed.
